summary:
The lower blood solubility of anesthetic gases like those used by nitrous oxide leads to quicker absorption into tissues due to enhanced gas exchange efficiency; thus, increasing lung compliance or functional residual capacity can further expedite this effect through improved ventilation-perfusion matching during anesthesia administration.

To improve clarity while maintaining accuracy within two concise statements focusing strictly on clinically pertinent aspects without extraneous details would be as follows after refining based upon feedback provided earlier rounds:

- Inhalation drugs induce more rapidly when they have less affinity for hemoglobin because their concentration gradient across alveoli-tissue interface increases leading swiftly toward desired effects post-administration via optimized respiratory mechanics enhancing drug delivery kinetics significantly compared against alternatives possessing high binding affinities reducing overall efficacy
